key_highlights: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after White House bailout collapses, stranding thousands of travelers and triggering industry-wide disruption.
Iran war continues to escalate with Hormuz blockade, driving oil prices and gas costs sharply higher ($4.39/gallon one-day jump).
OPEC+ agrees symbolic quota hike, but UAE exits the cartel amid economic strain from the blockade.
US jobs report expected to show resilience despite geopolitical turmoil, but market sentiment is cautious.
Berkshire Hathaway's cash pile surges under new CEO Greg Abel, signaling defensive posture.
Jamie Dimon warns next credit crisis will be 'worse than people expect', adding to risk-off tone.
Gold and silver prices slipping this week, while Bitcoin and Ethereum move higher, showing mixed safe-haven flows.
Ford focuses on affordability, Nike slashes 1,400 jobs, and consumer staples like General Mills and Conagra get analyst attention.
Vietnam inflation quickens due to energy costs from Iran war, impacting emerging markets.
Meta faces a New Mexico trial that could force major platform changes, while Mark Zuckerberg sends a startling message to employees.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surging due to Iran war and Hormuz blockade; OPEC+ quota hike symbolic but UAE exit signals discord; gas prices hit $4.39/gallon with biggest one-day jump since ceasefire announcement.
sectors.implications: Inflationary pressures intensify globally, especially for transport and manufacturing. Energy stocks may benefit short-term, but prolonged conflict risks demand destruction. ADNOC's $55B project awards post-OPEC indicate long-term supply investment.

sectors.trend: Spirit Airlines shuts down after bailout collapses, stranding thousands; other airlines (Duffy) assist customers; Royal Caribbean Q1 earnings show cruise demand but higher fuel costs weigh.
sectors.implications: Industry consolidation likely, with low-cost carriers most vulnerable. Higher fuel costs and labor shortages persist. Travel demand remains resilient but capacity constraints may push fares higher.

sectors.trend: Ford focuses on affordability; Nike slashes 1,400 jobs; General Mills, Conagra, and Procter & Gamble get analyst commentary; consumer spending under pressure from inflation.
sectors.implications: Down-trading to value brands and private labels likely. Companies with pricing power (e.g., PG) may hold margins better. Auto sector faces demand headwinds from high rates and fuel costs.

sectors.trend: Meta faces New Mexico trial that could force platform changes; Zuckerberg sends startling message to employees; Reddit Q1 earnings highlight ad revenue growth; Anthropic launches Claude Security for enterprises.
sectors.implications: Regulatory risk for big tech is rising. AI and cybersecurity remain bright spots. Meta's legal overhang could weigh on sentiment, but enterprise AI spending continues to grow.

sectors.trend: Jamie Dimon warns of next credit crisis being 'worse than expected'; Aegon and Barclays say prepare for market pain; Berkshire Hathaway's cash pile surges under Abel; high-yield savings and CD rates offer up to 4.1% APY.
sectors.implications: Banks are tightening lending standards and building reserves. Defensive positioning is evident. Rising deposit rates attract savers but compress bank margins. Credit risk is the key watch factor.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates moved higher in April; HELOC and home equity loan rates remain low; Ryman Hospitality Q1 earnings show hotel demand but cost pressures.
sectors.implications: Housing affordability remains challenged. Home equity lending is competitive as homeowners tap equity. Commercial real estate (especially office) faces refinancing risks.

sectors.trend: Caterpillar gets data center catalyst from Jim Cramer; Parsons Q1 earnings highlight government contracts; ADES secures offshore drilling contract in Nigeria; Thyssenkrupp and Jindal Steel pause talks.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical tensions boost defense and infrastructure spending. Data center buildout supports industrial demand. Energy infrastructure investment is accelerating.

sectors.trend: Vietnam inflation quickens due to energy costs; Philippines evacuates thousands as Mayon Volcano erupts; China tells firms to ignore US sanctions on refiners; Japan's Katayama declines to comment on FX intervention.
sectors.implications: EM currencies under pressure from strong USD and higher oil import bills. Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions add to inflation. Selective opportunities in energy and commodities.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran war and Hormuz blockade driving energy prices and inflation.
Airlines in crisis: Spirit shutdown highlights fragility of low-cost carriers.
Central bank divergence: Fed likely to hold rates higher for longer given inflation persistence.
Corporate earnings mixed: Consumer staples show resilience, but discretionary spending weakens.
Regulatory crackdown: Meta trial, FCC review, and medical credit card scrutiny.
Defensive positioning: Berkshire cash pile, Dimon warning, and safe-haven flows into cash and short-dated bonds.
Energy transition vs. energy security: OPEC+ discord and ADNOC investment signal long-term supply shifts.
AI and cybersecurity remain growth pockets despite broader market caution.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy producers (XOM, CVX) and reduce positions in transport and consumer discretionary. Hedge with oil futures or energy ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Spirit Airlines Collapse / Airline Sector Contagion
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid low-cost carriers; favor legacy airlines with strong balance sheets (DAL, UAL). Monitor for further bankruptcies and capacity cuts.
Reduce exposure to regional banks; favor money-center banks (JPM, BAC) with diversified revenue. Increase cash and short-duration Treasuries.
Favor TIPS, floating-rate bonds, and value stocks with pricing power. Avoid long-duration bonds and high-growth tech with no earnings.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory Risk for Big Tech (Meta Trial)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce overweight in Meta; diversify into other tech subsectors (AI, cybersecurity). Monitor trial outcomes for potential forced changes.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer Spending Slowdown / Job Cuts (Nike, Spirit)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Underweight consumer discretionary; overweight consumer staples (GIS, CAG, PG) and discount retailers. Focus on companies with strong balance sheets.
Maintain diversified EM exposure; favor Japan (EWJ) over China. Monitor South China Sea developments.
Expect higher oil price volatility. Favor integrated oil majors over pure-play E&P. Consider long-term positions in renewable energy as hedge.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to energy producers and oil services companies.
opportunities.rationale: Iran war and Hormuz blockade are structurally bullish for oil prices. OPEC+ discord and UAE exit add supply uncertainty. ADNOC's $55B investment signals long-term demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy consumer staples with pricing power (General Mills, Conagra, Procter & Gamble).
opportunities.rationale: Inflation and consumer down-trading favor value brands. These companies have strong margins and dividend yields, providing defensive income.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in AI and cybersecurity (Anthropic, Reddit, Parsons).
opportunities.rationale: Enterprise AI spending remains robust. Cybersecurity is a priority amid rising digital threats. Reddit's ad revenue growth shows platform resilience.

opportunities.recommendation: Take advantage of high-yield savings and CD rates (up to 4.1% APY) for cash reserves.
opportunities.rationale: Risk-off environment favors liquidity. Lock in attractive risk-free returns while waiting for better entry points in equities.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Berkshire Hathaway as a defensive core holding.
opportunities.rationale: Record cash pile under Greg Abel signals disciplined capital allocation. Diversified holdings provide stability in volatile markets.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to low-cost airlines and consumer discretionary stocks.
defensive_moves.rationale: Spirit shutdown and Nike job cuts indicate sector weakness. Higher fuel costs and slowing demand will pressure margins.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against credit risk by reducing regional bank exposure and increasing cash.
defensive_moves.rationale: Dimon's warning and Aegon/Barclays commentary suggest credit deterioration ahead. Regional banks are most vulnerable to commercial real estate losses.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Short duration bonds and TIPS to protect against inflation and rate hikes.
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation remains elevated due to energy costs. Short-dated Treasuries and TIPS provide real yield protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to Meta and other social media stocks facing regulatory risk.
defensive_moves.rationale: New Mexico trial could force significant platform changes, impacting ad revenue and user engagement. Zuckerberg's message suggests internal turmoil.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid long-duration real estate and mortgage REITs.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising mortgage rates and higher-for-longer Fed stance pressure housing affordability and REIT valuations. Commercial real estate refinancing risk is elevated.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. The Iran war and Hormuz blockade will keep energy prices elevated, stoking inflation and delaying Fed rate cuts. Spirit shutdown and Dimon's credit warning add to risk-off sentiment. Expect further downside in consumer discretionary and regional banks. Safe havens (cash, short-dated Treasuries, energy) will outperform. The US jobs report may show resilience but won't change the hawkish Fed stance.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic if geopolitical tensions de-escalate. Energy transition investments (ADNOC, renewables) will accelerate. AI and cybersecurity remain structural growth themes. Consumer staples and Berkshire offer defensive compounding. However, a credit crisis or prolonged Iran conflict could trigger a recession. Diversification and quality are key.
key_catalysts: Iran ceasefire negotiations and Hormuz blockade resolution.
US jobs report (May 3) and subsequent Fed commentary.
OPEC+ June meeting and quota decisions post-UAE exit.
Meta trial outcome and potential regulatory changes.
Spirit Airlines bankruptcy proceedings and industry consolidation.
Jamie Dimon's credit crisis warnings and bank earnings.
Nike job cuts and consumer spending data.
Vietnam inflation and EM currency stability.
Berkshire Hathaway's Q1 13F filing for portfolio clues.
Anthropic and Reddit earnings for AI/advertising trends.
watch_list: WTI and Brent crude oil prices (key inflation input).
US 10-year Treasury yield and 2s10s spread (recession signal).
US dollar index (DXY) vs. EM currencies.
Gasoline prices at the pump (consumer sentiment).
Bitcoin and gold price divergence (safe-haven flows).
Meta stock price and trial developments.
Spirit Airlines customer refunds and DOT actions.
